_meta.table_status: Breakdown Table (21.38) not yet extracted. BD accumulation and column detection are fully testable; dice roll is stubbed.

cpa_system.description: Rule 6.13: CPA is how many CP a unit may use before earning DPs
cpa_system.note: A Tank Battalion with CPA 25 can use exactly 25 CP in an OpStage without earning DPs.

cpa_system.description: Rule 6.11: Gun units with CPA '0' are treated as CPA 10 for non-movement purposes
cpa_system.note: CPA 0 gun units cannot move; they use CPA 10 only for combat/other purposes.

cpa_system.description: Rule 6.15: Formation CPA = lowest CPA of constituent units
cpa_system.example: British 7th Armored Division moves at CPA 10 (its lowest-CPA unit, the 1st KRRC without trucks, at CPA 10)
cpa_system.direction: UPWARD — units determine formation CPA, not the reverse

cpa_system.description: Rule 6.16: Unused CPs do not carry over to next OpStage
cpa_system.note: CP allowance resets each OpStage. Not transferable between units.

dp_from_cpa_excess.description: Rule 6.21 example: CPA 15, uses 18 CP → 3 DPs
Rule 6.21: CPA 8, 1st RNF uses 5+5=10 CP → 2 DPs
dp_from_cpa_excess.note: From the 1st RNF example in rule 6.22.

non_motorized_cap.description: Rule 8.17: CPA 8 non-mot may never voluntarily exceed 50% over base = 12 CP
non_motorized_cap.note: 8 + 50% of 8 = 12. Applies ONLY to voluntary movement in own portion of OpStage.

non_motorized_cap.description: Rule 8.17: CPA 10 non-mot may never voluntarily exceed 15 CP

non_motorized_cap.condition: CPA <= 10 identifies non-motorized units for this cap

non_motorized_cap.description: Rule 8.17: cap does NOT apply to Reaction or Retreat Before Assault
non_motorized_cap.note: 'Reaction and Retreat Before Assault are not voluntary movement in your own portion of an Operations Stage; they occur in the other player's portion'

zoc_movement.description: Rule 8.14: unit entering enemy ZOC must stop immediately
zoc_movement.note: 'If a unit moves into an enemy Zone of Control it must stop immediately. It may move no further in that Movement Segment.'

zoc_movement.description: Rule 10.22: no CP cost to enter enemy ZOC
zoc_movement.note: The terrain entry cost still applies; there is no extra ZOC surcharge to enter.

zoc_movement.description: Rule 8.15 (2): exiting Contact ZOC costs 2 CP
Rule 8.15 (3): exiting Engaged ZOC costs 4 CP
Rule 8.15 prerequisite: unit must begin segment in enemy ZOC to use this rule
zoc_movement.note: Rule 8.15 applies when 'a unit begins a Movement Segment in an enemy Zone of Control'

fuel_consumption.description: Rule 49.13: fuel rate '4', 12 CP moved → 3 groups of 5 → 3 × 4 = 12 fuel
fuel_consumption.note: 'The fuel consumption factor is the number of Fuel Points consumed for every five Capability Points (or fraction thereof) expended for movement.'

fuel_consumption.description: Rule 49.13: 'fraction thereof' → 1 CP movement still costs fuel (ceil(1/5)=1 group)
Rule 49.13: 6 CP movement = ceil(6/5) = 2 groups → 2 × rate
Rule 49.13: fuel NOT consumed for combat CP expenditure
fuel_consumption.note: 'Fuel is not consumed when CPs are expended for combat or any other action that does not involve actual movement from one hex to another.'
25 × (1/5) × 4 = 20. A unit always has exactly enough fuel capacity to expend its full CPA on movement.

breakdown_accumulation.description: Rule 21.21: each hex terrain has a Breakdown Point Value from TEC
breakdown_accumulation.note: BD values from hex_map.entry_bd(). BD accumulates when moving into the hex.

breakdown_accumulation.description: Rule 21.23: all vehicles in a stack accumulate BD for terrain moved through
breakdown_accumulation.note: BD is per-hex, not per-unit. All units in a formation moving through the same hexes accumulate the same BD.

breakdown_accumulation.description: Rule 21.25: BD points are cumulative within an OpStage (across both players' portions)
breakdown_accumulation.note: BD from Reaction, Retreat, and voluntary movement all accumulate. BD does NOT reset mid-stage.

breakdown_accumulation.description: Rule 21.27: no breakdown check until BD > 3
breakdown_accumulation.note: 'A unit does not check for Breakdown until it has accumulated more than three Breakdown Points'

breakdown_accumulation.description: Rule 21.26: once a check is made, next check only needed if BD crosses into next table column

example.note: 15 and 17 are both in the 11-20 column → no second check needed.

breakdown_accumulation.description: Rule 21.26: BD crosses into new column → check required

breakdown_columns.note: Rule 21.33: units accumulating > 71 BD use the 71+ column. Column 0 = no check (< 4 BD).
